items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: You've come, Warrior.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Huh? Are you drinking...?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Just wheat juice!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: ...Sure, wheat juice.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ah, today you...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: You smell nice. Like dried wheat. Warm.

options.text: Do you really have a wolf's sense of smell?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Well.. that's just what I think! When I was little, I used to roll in the wheat fields.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: My-my-my-my Forte gave me the tail, but it seems my nose is keener than most.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Haha... it's good to have {PlayerName} by my side!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Lupa, you've had enough wheat juice. Sit. Take a break for a moment.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Even though it's over, there's still a heaviness. A drink helps.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I've sent word to the Ephor's Palace about Domina Julia, but finding the Silvas that fled will take time.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Four years ago, Augusta won the title of champion. Since becoming Ephor, much of the corruption tied to the arena has been cleared.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: If Mya had lived, perhaps things would have been different.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Sometimes I wonder... What would my life have been like if my parents hadn't handed me over to the Silvas when we first came to Septimont?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Just like one of those stories, with the different endings...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Have you ever thought about living a different life, Lupa?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Haha... No need. I'm content with what I have.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: For everything gained, something is lost. But through it all, I met you. That's enough.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Life is an arena. Victory and defeat are a part of that.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: No one wins forever. The more victories, the higher the expectation, and the heavier the weight of defeat.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Do you fear that one day... you might face defeat?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: ...You seem to live to meet others' expectations. This is what you're used to, I'd say.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ha, that suits you well! To move forward without the burden of a lofty vow is a lighter path.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Even if one day you meet defeat, remember this. Those you've helped will never let you face it alone.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I may not have your strength, but should you call, I will meet you on the battlefield. No matter the distance.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I swear it by the eternal sun, my fellow warrior.
